premises.label: P1
premises.statement: Norms can hold without coercive enforcement

premises.label: P2
premises.statement: We can observe that A causes B, not merely that A and B co-occur

premises.label: P3
premises.statement: There is a coherent rational agent stable enough to ground theory

premises.label: P4
premises.statement: Who owns what is prior to politics; politics distributes within those facts

premises.label: P5
premises.statement: We can reason about principles by abstracting from class and interest

premises.label: P6
premises.statement: Correct analysis enables deliberate production of better arrangements

premises.label: P7
premises.statement: Historical development has an immanent logic that can be known

premises.label: P8
premises.statement: Human nature has a stable end that determines what counts as flourishing
